item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You ruined pickles for me forever.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this was made specifically to make me feel ill. +1, but I hate you for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The living pickle - Dr Popov.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so well written that it's going to be a while before I eat a pickle again. Take my upvote and go away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is literally more terrifying than the skips that tear your guts apart or eat the universe or whatever. This is absolutely disgusting and I hate it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was immediately intrigued by the classifications in the header; keter/1/1 does not make a lot of sense, and I was curious if the article could justify it. It does, and is quite good on top of that.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, well-written, and makes me horrified that I might have eaten a slug before.
The ending wasn't as fleshed out as I thought it was going to be, but it works.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What have you done you monster?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yeah, this works way better now. Everything feels much more grounded. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it weird that I feel bad for the pickles?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks a lot for the crit! I've made a number of changes based on your suggestions, including a complete rewrite of the containment procedures.

Hopefully this improves the lead-in to the experiment log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Gee, why would they comment on the nutritional conte—OH NO."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gross. Gross, gross, gross.

Low-level but widespread antimemetic/cognitohazard stuff is some of my favorite, and you make full use of it here. Job well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im split on this, while its really well writen and makes me never want to eat pickels again, its very short and could be expanded on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think this needs expanding at all. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a novote for me, but not an especially critical one. It's a rare time where I feel the article does exactly what it sets out to do, but still isn't quite enough for a +1.

Quick crit!
Crit, quick!

"Products identified as being infested with SCP-4781 are to be recalled." The 'identified as being infested with' bugs me a bit. You can condense down to 'All products infested with…' .

"Biohazard teams are to seize the facility they were bred and packaged in" mixing tenses here. You also never really go into detail where these things come from. Are they still breeding? Who bred them? Where's this facility? Why did the breeding stop? If it's the only place these things come from, then once the recall is complete will they be neutralized?

"They are non-toxic but lacking nutritionally, generally providing three grams of carbohydrates each." I can never get behind starting a conproc or description sentence with "they". Either condense with the previous sentence, or say "Instances" or "Organisms" or just "SCP-4781".

Also, more nitpicky; "Due to their current proliferation, it is estimated 38% of humans have been exposed to a living instance." I have to guess you mean out of the human population who have eaten non-anomalous pickles before. 38% of the human species is a HUGE number, especially including areas of the world where pickles aren't commonplace. Even assuming you just mean 38% of the population who eat pickles, that's still enormous, especially since you said in the con procs "facility" not "facilities" or "factories".

Overall, this is disgusting; It evokes disgust, it achieves disgust. Taken on it's own, the article is a success. The problem is, the containment and description don't enhance this sentiment. Little touches, such as basic measurements, visual elements, or as ARD noted, evolutionary or biological factors could make the anomaly feel more grounded in reality. All of these things could have been included without adding too much to the WC.

item.title: Verdict: Ew
item.content:encoded:
I have a family member who's disgusted by pickles. Finds them revolting. I always thought they were weird, since no one else in the family feels the same way.

Having read your SCP, I have a better understanding of their disgust. Thanks for putting me off of pickles for…. okay, probably about a day or so.

Definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Note for myself: Don't eat pickles again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While this certainly could have been expanded on further, I don't think it needed to be. It used a small wordcount to elicit some seriously disgusting vibes and I rather enjoyed that.

D-41562 consumes each half in several bites. Some entrails fall out of their mouth and onto the table, which they then clean up.

Cause oh man did this make me never want to eat a pickle ever again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
aismallard, I would forever hold contempt against you for making me imagine the act of eating live sludge, and making me upvote an article containing such depiction.

Thanks, I hate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This raises some disturbing questions. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My exact thoughts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot of room for expansion here. How did such an anomaly evolve? Do humans and pickle slugs share a semi-symbiotic relationship? Is there an organic vegetable company that just breeds and sells these things? The basic effect of slugs disguising themselves as pickles is enough for a gross experiment log, but it would work even better as a capstone for a How It's Made segment about pickles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I didn't need this, but thanks again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty well written, almost too well written for a downvote, but I feel as if it alone isn't enough to justify such a short article. The grossout style is good, but it feels underdeveloped, and ends very quickly. The ending didn't really catch me off guard or do anything for me that the rest of the article didn't do, and that's why I'm downvoting.

Edit: actually, on a second readthrough, I can't ignore how well written it is, even if the ending isn't as satisfying as I'd like, so I'm replacing my downvote with a novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit. The imagery of the experiment log here is excellent.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Help I brought a bunch of pickles home and now I can't bring myself to eat them.

Thanks to wctaiwan, Rounderhouse and Lambda Fairy for critiquing this!
The image is an original creation of the author, licensed as CC-BY-SA 3.0.
